title: Project Administrator (m/f/d)
location: Germany (ABB – Motion High Power Division Germany)
required_skills: At least 2 years relevant professional experience in commercial project handling
Proficiency in written and spoken English and German
bonus_skills: Additional languages beyond English and German
matched_skills: German language skills (B1 — borderline, role likely requires C1+)
Financial tracking and reporting (USD 8M programme budget at HSBC)
Documentation and reporting support (Hapag-Lloyd, ECE Group)
missing_skills: SAP knowledge (no SAP mentioned anywhere in CV)
Commercial/contractual project administration experience (invoicing, purchase orders, revenue recognition)
Experience with customer orders, guarantees/bonds, accounts receivable monitoring
Technical commercial education or Kaufmännische Ausbildung background
core_responsibilities: Entry and maintenance of order-related data in SAP (SOX compliance)
Invoicing of customer orders per revenue recognition requirements
Commercial and contractual review of customer orders (Incoterms, payment terms)
Monitoring accounts receivable with Project Management and SAS
recommended_emphasis: Highlight the financial tracking and budget variance reporting experience from HSBC (USD 8M programme) as evidence of financial governance discipline
Emphasise any Excel/data entry work from ECE Group or Hapag-Lloyd that demonstrates systematic data management
German language skills are critical — note B1 level honestly; this role likely requires B2/C1 for contractual German communication
Do not apply without SAP experience — this is listed as 'Advanced knowledge' and is a hard requirement
summary: Match score is 28.6 (weak_match). The candidate has relevant financial reporting and stakeholder coordination experience, but lacks the core technical requirements: advanced SAP, commercial/contractual project administration, and the specific billing/invoicing workflow experience ABB requires. German at B1 may also be insufficient for a role involving contractual and financial communications. Not recommended unless candidate can demonstrate SAP exposure.
